EcoVadis Assessment Excellence

Companies across global supply chains are increasingly required to complete an assessment to demonstrate responsible business practices. Buyers, investors, and partners use these ratings to evaluate ESG performance.

The Challenge

Completing the assessment successfully requires structured documentation and clear policies. Many organizations struggle to interpret the questionnaire or submit the right evidence to achieve a passing score.

The Expert Advantage

Our experts help companies navigate the process with confidence. From readiness evaluation to policy development, we guide you step-by-step to improve performance and strengthen your rating.

Our EcoVadis Assessment Consulting Services

Our experts provide end-to-end support to help organizations complete the process efficiently and strengthen sustainability performance.

We conduct a structured evaluation of your sustainability policies, management systems, and documentation to identify gaps before submission.

Policy Development and Documentation

• Environmental policy
• Human rights policy
• Ethical business conduct policy
• Sustainable procurement policy
Clear documentation significantly improves evaluation outcomes.

Questionnaire Guidance

Our specialists guide organizations in completing the questionnaire accurately while ensuring responses reflect actual sustainability practices.

Evidence and Documentation Support

We assist in organizing and submitting the required supporting documents to strengthen credibility.

After submission, we develop a roadmap to improve sustainability performance and increase the likelihood of a stronger rating in future evaluations.

Our Step-by-Step EcoVadis Support Process

We follow a structured methodology designed to simplify the evaluation process.

1

Initial Consultation

Understand your organization’s sustainability practices and objectives.

2

Gap Analysis

Evaluate existing policies and identify areas requiring improvement.

3

Policy and Documentation Development

4

Questionnaire Completion

Support teams in completing the submission accurately.

5

Score Improvement Strategy

Book a Free Sustainability Consultation

What Is EcoVadis Assessment?

The EcoVadis assessment is a globally recognized sustainability rating system used by companies to evaluate suppliers and partners on environmental, social, and governance (ESG) performance.

The platform assesses companies across four key sustainability themes:

• Environment
• Labor and Human Rights
• Ethics
• Sustainable Procurement

Organizations submit documentation, policies, and supporting evidence through the questionnaire. Based on the evaluation, companies receive a score and may qualify for medals such as Bronze, Silver, Gold, or Platinum.

Today, thousands of multinational companies rely on sustainability ratings to monitor supplier performance and ensure responsible sourcing practices.

Completing the process effectively allows businesses to:

• Strengthen supply chain relationships
• Demonstrate ESG commitment
• Meet buyer sustainability requirements
• Improve transparency and risk management

Talk to our Expert Today

Why Companies Choose Growlity for EcoVadis Assessment

Most companies already have sustainability initiatives in place.
What they lack is a structured system that EcoVadis can evaluate confidently.

That is why many organizations receive lower scores than expected despite investing in ESG activities.

At Growlity, we help companies transform scattered sustainability efforts into audit-ready, score-focused ESG systems aligned with EcoVadis methodology.

From policy development and documentation structuring to questionnaire guidance and score improvement strategy, our experts provide end-to-end support designed to strengthen both sustainability performance and supplier credibility.

Contact us

Why EcoVadis Assessment Matters for Businesses

Sustainability is no longer optional. Global organizations are integrating ESG performance into procurement decisions and supplier evaluation frameworks.

Completing the assessment allows companies to demonstrate that their operations align with international sustainability expectations.

Key benefits include:

Supply Chain Access

Many multinational companies require suppliers to complete sustainability ratings before onboarding.

Improved Corporate Credibility

A strong sustainability score helps build trust with customers, partners, and investors.

Risk Management

Structured sustainability policies help reduce operational, environmental, and social risks.

Competitive Advantage

Companies with higher sustainability ratings often stand out during supplier selection.
Organizations that proactively strengthen ESG practices are better positioned to meet evolving regulatory and market expectations.

Improve Your Score with Expert Guidance

Common Challenges Companies Face in Assessment

Although the process appears straightforward, many organizations face difficulties when completing the questionnaire and preparing documentation.
Common challenges include:

1

Lack of Required Policies

Many companies do not have formal sustainability policies aligned with international frameworks.

2

Difficulty Understanding the Questionnaire

The questionnaire requires detailed information about sustainability practices and evidence.

3

Insufficient Documentation

Supporting evidence must be clear, structured, and aligned with ESG expectations.

4

Low Initial Scores

Organizations often receive low ratings due to gaps in governance or missing sustainability initiatives.

5

Uncertainty About Score Improvement

Many companies do not know which actions will significantly improve their sustainability rating.

Case Study: Improving a Supplier Sustainability Score

A manufacturing company supplying to global buyers faced challenges during the evaluation process. The organization lacked structured ESG documentation and received a low initial rating.

Our consulting team conducted a sustainability gap analysis and supported the company in developing environmental and human rights policies, improving internal governance practices, and organizing documentation.

Within one assessment cycle, the company improved its sustainability score significantly and strengthened relationships with international clients.

This demonstrates how structured sustainability management can improve supplier credibility and market opportunities.

Get Your Score Improvement Plan

Understanding EcoVadis Score Levels and Medal Recognition

How Scores and Medals Work

The EcoVadis assessment evaluates companies using a sustainability scoring system ranging from 0 to 100, based on performance across four ESG themes: Environment, Labor & Human Rights, Ethics, and Sustainable Procurement.

In addition to the score, companies may receive medals that recognize top-performing organizations relative to all companies evaluated globally.

Since 2024–2025, medals are awarded based on percentile ranking rather than fixed score thresholds. This means a company’s recognition depends on how it performs compared with other companies in the database over the previous 12 months.

Current percentile recognition levels include:

Platinum Medal

Top 1% of companies

Gold Medal

Top 5% of companies

Silver Medal

Top 15% of companie

Bronze Medal

Top 35% of companies

Companies outside these percentile ranges may still receive a scorecard and improvement recommendations, which can be used to strengthen sustainability performance in future assessments.

This percentile approach ensures that sustainability recognition reflects current global performance benchmarks rather than static thresholds.

 Talk to our experts today.

Scoring System

EcoVadis Badges

Committed

A minimum score of 45

Fast Mover

A score between 34-44

EcoVadis FAQs *{ box-sizing:border-box; margin:0; padding:0; } body{ font-family:‘Plus Jakarta Sans’,sans-serif; } .FAQ-section{ padding:20px 20px 100px; background:#f8fafc; } .ev-faq-container{ max-width:1200px; margin:0 auto; } .ev-faq-intro{ text-align:center; margin-bottom:50px; } .ev-faq-subtitle{ display:inline-block; padding:6px 16px; background:rgba(0,184,124,0.1); border:1px solid rgba(0,184,124,0.2); border-radius:50px; color:#00B87C; font-size:14px; font-weight:700; text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:1px; } .ev-h2-blue{ font-size:clamp(32px,5vw,42px); font-weight:800; color:#0b72e7; margin-top:15px; line-height:1.2; } .ev-faq-list{ max-width:900px; margin:0 auto; display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:16px; } .ev-faq-item{ background:#ffffff; border:1px solid #e2e8f0; border-radius:16px; overflow:hidden; transition:all 0.3s ease; box-shadow:0 4px 6px -1px rgba(0,0,0,0.05); } .ev-faq-item:hover{ transform:translateY(-2px); box-shadow:0 12px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.08); } .ev-faq-question{ width:100%; padding:24px 30px; display:flex; justify-content:space-between; align-items:center; cursor:pointer; background:transparent; border:none; text-align:left; transition:background 0.3s ease; } .ev-faq-h3{ font-size:18px; font-weight:700; color:#1e293b; line-height:1.4; } .ev-faq-icon{ font-size:22px; color:#0b72e7; transition:transform 0.4s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); flex-shrink:0; line-height:1; } .ev-faq-answer{ max-height:0; overflow:hidden; opacity:0; will-change:max-height; transition: max-height 0.4s ease, opacity 0.3s ease; } .ev-faq-item.active .ev-faq-answer{ opacity:1; } .ev-faq-answer-inner{ padding:0 30px 30px; } .ev-faq-answer-inner p{ font-size:16px; line-height:1.7; color:#475569; margin-bottom:15px; } .ev-faq-answer-inner p:last-child{ margin-bottom:0; } .ev-faq-answer-inner ul{ margin:10px 0 15px 20px; color:#475569; font-size:16px; line-height:1.7; } .ev-faq-answer-inner strong{ color:#1e293b; font-weight:700; } .faq-cta-link{ color:#0b72e7; font-weight:700; text-decoration:none; } .faq-cta-link:hover{ text-decoration:underline; } .ev-faq-item.active{ border-color:#0b72e7; box-shadow:0 10px 25px -5px rgba(11,114,231,0.1); } .ev-faq-item.active .ev-faq-h3{ color:#0b72e7; } .ev-faq-item.active .ev-faq-icon{ transform:rotate(45deg); color:#00B87C; } @media (max-width:768px){ .ev-faq-question{ padding:18px 16px; } .ev-faq-answer-inner{ padding:0 16px 24px; } .ev-faq-h3{ font-size:16px; } .ev-h2-blue{ font-size:28px; } }

Common Questions

EcoVadis Assessment Frequently Asked Questions

What is an EcoVadis assessment?

An EcoVadis assessment is a sustainability evaluation system used by companies worldwide to measure the environmental, social, and ethical performance of businesses across global supply chains.

During the process, companies complete a structured questionnaire and submit supporting documentation that demonstrates their sustainability practices, policies, and management systems.

The assessment evaluates organizations across key ESG areas such as environmental impact, labor practices, business ethics, and responsible sourcing.

Need help completing your assessment? Book an EcoVadis Consultation →

What are the 4 pillars of EcoVadis?

The EcoVadis assessment framework evaluates companies across four sustainability pillars.

Environment
Environmental management systems, carbon emissions, waste management, and climate initiatives.

Labor and Human Rights
Employee safety, diversity, working conditions, and human rights protection.

Ethics
Anti-corruption, governance, compliance, and responsible business conduct.

Sustainable Procurement
Supplier sustainability management and responsible sourcing practices.

How does the EcoVadis calculation work?

EcoVadis scores are calculated using a weighted assessment of sustainability policies, actions, and measurable results.

  • Policy commitments
  • Implementation actions
  • Performance indicators
  • Supporting evidence

Companies may receive Bronze, Silver, Gold, or Platinum recognition depending on final performance.

Where can I find the EcoVadis questionnaire?

The EcoVadis questionnaire becomes available after registration on the EcoVadis platform.

The questionnaire is customized based on company size, industry, and operational geography.

Companies must provide sustainability policies, certifications, and supporting ESG documentation.

What kind of questions are included in an EcoVadis assessment?

EcoVadis assessment questions focus on sustainability management practices and ESG performance.

  • Environmental management
  • Carbon reduction initiatives
  • Employee health and safety
  • Human rights practices
  • Anti-corruption policies
  • Responsible sourcing

Strong documentation and evidence are essential for achieving higher scores.

What Is the EcoVadis 360° Watch Monitoring System?

EcoVadis 360° Watch is an AI-driven monitoring system that tracks sustainability-related controversies and ESG risks linked to companies.

  • Corruption allegations
  • Labor violations
  • Environmental incidents
  • ESG-related legal disputes

Significant controversies may affect a company’s EcoVadis rating or medal eligibility.

document.addEventListener(‘DOMContentLoaded’, () => { const faqItems = document.querySelectorAll(‘.ev-faq-item’); faqItems.forEach(item => { const trigger = item.querySelector(‘.ev-faq-question’); trigger.addEventListener(‘click’, () => { const isActive = item.classList.contains(‘active’); faqItems.forEach(otherItem => { otherItem.classList.remove(‘active’); const otherAnswer = otherItem.querySelector(‘.ev-faq-answer’); otherItem .querySelector(‘.ev-faq-question’) .setAttribute(‘aria-expanded’,‘false’); if(otherAnswer){ otherAnswer.style.maxHeight = null; } }); if(!isActive){ item.classList.add(‘active’); trigger.setAttribute(‘aria-expanded’,‘true’); const answer = item.querySelector(‘.ev-faq-answer’); if(answer){ answer.style.maxHeight = answer.scrollHeight + “px”; } } }); }); /* Open first FAQ by default */ const firstItem = faqItems[0]; if(firstItem){ firstItem.classList.add(‘active’); const firstAnswer = firstItem.querySelector(‘.ev-faq-answer’); const firstTrigger = firstItem.querySelector(‘.ev-faq-question’); firstTrigger.setAttribute(‘aria-expanded’,‘true’); firstAnswer.style.maxHeight = firstAnswer.scrollHeight + “px”; } });
